model.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: UConn holds a meaningful ~6-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage over Louisville (KenPom #11, SRS 22.92 vs. Louisville's KenPom #18, AdjEM +25.42), anchored by an elite defense ranked 24th nationally (DRtg 97.9) that is well-positioned to challenge a Cardinals offense ranked 23rd nationally. Louisville's own elite two-way profile — top-23 on both offense and defense — and the uncertainty surrounding Mikel Brown Jr.'s back injury recovery make this genuinely competitive, and UConn's late-season fragility (two losses in four games, a 72-52 blowout to St. John's in the Big East final, Hurley's public concerns about turnovers) caps confidence meaningfully. However, UConn's superior overall efficiency margin, Big East battle-testing, and the tournament pedigree of a two-time defending champion program under Dan Hurley ultimately tip this Sweet 16 matchup to the Huskies, even as Louisville's defensive excellence and potential full health for Brown give the Cardinals a credible upset path.

reasoningStep.title: Louisville vs. UConn Sweet 16 — Efficiency, Health, and Form Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: UConn holds a ~6-point KenPom AdjEM edge and an elite defense ranked 24th nationally, but their late-season form concerns (2 losses in 4 games, turnover issues, Hurley fragility comments) and offensive limitations (ORtg 116.5, 54th nationally) create genuine upset risk. Louisville's two-way defensive excellence (top-23 on both ends), ACC battle-testing, and Mikel Brown Jr.'s potential return to full health make them a credible threat. UConn's superior overall efficiency profile and tournament pedigree tip the pick to the Huskies at moderate confidence.
evidence: Louisville: KenPom #18, AdjEM +25.42, AdjOE 124.1 (20th), AdjDE 98.7 (23rd); Torvik #16, AdjOE 124.0 (23rd), AdjDE 98.1 (23rd). Record: 23-10 ACC.
UConn: 29-5 record, 17-3 Big East (2nd place), SRS 22.92 (12th nationally), ORtg 116.5 (54th), DRtg 97.9 (24th). Ranked 6th in AP Poll heading into tournament.

UConn suffered a 72-52 blowout loss to St. John's in the Big East final — their 2nd loss in 4 games. Hurley cited team fragility and turnover problems. Silas Demary Jr. had an apparent ankle injury in the Big East final but Hurley gave positive pre-tournament injury updates.
Mikel Brown Jr. (Louisville's #2 scorer, projected lottery pick) had a reaggravated back injury and missed ~4 games before the tournament; his Sweet 16 health status carries residual uncertainty.

UConn beat Furman (R64) and UCLA (R32) to reach the Sweet 16; Louisville beat South Florida (R64) and Michigan State (R32). UConn's R32 pick over UCLA carried 0.72 confidence with Demary expected to play.

I now have enough data to make a well-informed pick. Let me synthesize everything I've gathered:

**Louisville (6-seed):**
- KenPom #18, AdjEM +25.42; Torvik #16, AdjOE 124.0 (23rd), AdjDE 98.1 (23rd)
- 23-10 record, ACC battle-tested
- Mikel Brown Jr. (projected lottery pick, #2 scorer/top facilitator) had a reaggravated back injury and missed ~4 games heading into the tournament; the prior pick noted he was "targeted to return at full strength" for the first round vs. South Florida
- Beat South Florida (R64) and Michigan State (R32) to reach Sweet 16

**UConn (2-seed):**
- KenPom #11, SRS 22.92 (12th), ORtg 116.5 (54th), DRtg 97.9 (24th); 29-5 record, 17-3 in Big East (2nd place)
- Ranked 6th in AP Poll
- Jaylin Stewart: missed final 6 games with knee injury (bench role anyway)
- Silas Demary Jr.: apparent ankle injury in Big East Tournament final (72-52 loss to St. John's)
- Dan Hurley gave "positive injury updates" before the tournament — Demary expected to play
- UConn blew out Furman in R64, beat UCLA in R32 to reach Sweet 16
- Hurley expressed concerns about team "fragility" and turnover problems after the St. John's blowout loss
- Two losses in four games heading into the tournament

**Key matchup analysis:**
- UConn holds a ~6-point KenPom AdjEM advantage (#11 vs. #18), but Louisville's Torvik rank (#16) is actually very close to UConn's implied Torvik rank (~#7 per prior picks)
- UConn's defense (DRtg 97.9, 24th nationally) is elite and will challenge Louisville's offense
- Louisville's defense (AdjDE 98.1, 23rd nationally) is also elite — this is a defensive battle
- UConn's offense (ORtg 116.5, 54th nationally) is actually a relative weakness — Louisville's defense is well-positioned to exploit this
- Louisville's offense (AdjOE 124.0, 23rd nationally) is significantly better than UConn's offense
- The efficiency gap favors UConn overall, but it's not enormous (~6 pts KenPom)
- UConn's late-season form concerns (2 losses in 4 games, fragility concerns from Hurley, turnover issues) are real
- Mikel Brown Jr.'s health for the Sweet 16 is uncertain — he was "targeted to return" for R64 but his status for the Sweet 16 after two more games is unclear

The prior picks established UConn beat UCLA in R32 with Demary expected to play. UConn's superior overall efficiency profile, elite defense, and tournament pedigree (two-time defending champions) give them the edge, but Louisville's two-way defensive excellence and the possibility that Brown is now healthier after more recovery time make this genuinely competitive. UConn's ~6-point efficiency advantage and elite defense are the deciding factors.
